feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Build Process Automation
feed.subtitle: SAP Build Process Automation Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Error - Failed to start outlook, Internal Instance of outlook is null.
entry.content: I am creating a simple automation of outlook using this https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-outlook-automation.html#6d46ac9d-1797-4178-808d-8438c019d13b . But i'm getting error "Error - Failed to start outlook, Internal Instance of outlook is null. " when i'm testing the automation.

entry.title: Process Automation: Unattended agent is disconnected automatically
entry.content:
Hi all,

I created an automated process in SAP Build Process Automation. The process is started with a scheduled timer. The process works exactly fine when my computer is active and I can see that the agent is active.

However, when I do not have my computer "active", the agent is automatically put to disconnected. When I go to Monitor > Automated Jobs, I can see that the job is set to "Ready" with the comment "Your job cannot be distributed because no unattended agent is available". Also, if I put the agent to unattended and close my computer, I get the same error because the agent is set to "Disconnected".

Is it possible to let an agent run the process even though your computer is off?

Thanks,

Stefanie

entry.title: How to use Action in a Task automation
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I'm new to Business Process Automation (BPA) and appreciate for any advice on a couple of issues I'm facing:

Background:
I'm working on a project that involves extracting data from Excel and using it to generate a Purchase Requisition Document via an API.

What I've Done So Far:
I've successfully used the necessary API to create and publish an Action (see Figure 1).

The Problems I'm Encountering:

  1. I'm trying to use this Action within a Task automation process, but I'm having trouble locating the option to invoke the Action in the right-hand panel of the screen (as shown in Figure 2). 

  2. Instead within the task automation, I can called a Process, and within that Process, I can invoke the Action. My question is, how do I set the input and output parameters for this Process? (Figure 3)

Any help you can offer would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: I'm facing issue while registering for External Authentication in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: I'm facing issue while registering for External Authentication in SAP Build Process Automation. It returns a Blank screen and registration doesn't complete.

entry.title: SAP Build process automation
entry.content: Hi, I am new to SAP Build process automation. I want to complete the certification in BPA, please guide me how where can I learn everything related to course.

entry.title: Error while calling APIs from Build Process Automation
entry.content:
We are using Build Process Automation on BTP and downloaded the project Inactive Vendor Mark for Blockage or Deletion from the Store and trying to implement it. We have made the required configurations and created destinations, but facing error in Automation to Fetch Finance Document Details, while testing the automation: "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '__count')".  Please suggest.

Gave input as below

Error:

{
    "uid": "bd0d2b28-30f1-4897-a408-c653f4289b40",
    "name": "Automation to Fetch Finance Document Details",
    "type": "automation",
    "instanceUid": "b6f019e3-582e-4393-ba6c-49d257822f1e",
    "packageUid": "5d081521-2d4e-4b5a-88eb-f589fbc396ef",
    "packageVersionUid": "b533a81e-af05-45fd-9896-82b8aac16acb",
    "attended": true,
    "duration": 3,
    "status": "Failed",
    "code": "KO",
    "label": "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '__count')",
    "containerInstanceUid": "b6f019e3-582e-4393-ba6c-49d257822f1e",
    "parentInstanceUid": "8cdba4ef-3d44-28ad-9193-8c4005ccc152",
    "line": 2,
    "file": "bundle.js",
    "exception": "irpa_core.error.Error",
    "stack": "Error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '__count')    at e.error.cast (C:\Users\tbandaru\AppData\Local\SAP\Intelligent RPA\Projects\973e2db6-6d85-4b76-9646-22b67e2a275b\dist\bundle.js:2:208641)    at t.Job.start (C:\Users\tbandaru\AppData\Local\SAP\Intelligent RPA\Projects\973e2db6-6d85-4b76-9646-22b67e2a275b\dist\bundle.js:2:303965)    at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)"
}

entry.title: SAP Build - Desktop Agent 3 waiting for a project to be loaded
entry.content:
Hi, I'm doing the "Process and approve your invoices with SAP Build Process Automation" missison from SAP Discovery Center using a SAP BTP Trial account and Desktop Agent 3 Version 3.32.58

 

I make a copy of the sample project and modify it to not use the destination "sap_process_automation_document_store".

 

I did some test and it worked fine.

 

Testing again, the desktop agent get in a loop reading the invoice, so i had to cancel the instance.

 

After that, the Desktop Agent stop acepting projects.

 

I went to C:Users/Username/AppData/Local/SAP/Intelligent RPA and the "Projects" folder is missing.

 

How can I make the agent recognize projects?.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Process Automation BAPI Action Group - RFC_READ_TABLE
entry.content:
I'm trying to use this tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-bapi-actiongroup.html to create an action group to use the RFC_READ_TABLE FM from a SAP ERP connection.

But the issue i'm having is that i cannot retrieve information from this RFC. It only allows me to use BAPI's in the search.

Anyone had this kind of issue ? Is this a limitation of the action group artifact ? And if so, why the tutorial is pointing to this RFC FM ?

Thanks in advance for your help.

Regards,

Alexandre Pereira

entry.title: SAP BPA : Unable to open project
entry.content:
Hi,

Since the new release of SAP BPA today, I am unable to open any project, it just shows a blank white screen.

Is anyone else also facing the same issue?

Regards,

Himanshu

@Archana

entry.title: Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation: Subscription Failed
entry.content: I am trying out free trial, but when I tried to subscribe to "SAP Build Process Automation" it failed with a message "Couldn't subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation: Subscription Failed" tried twice and failed both the time. It does not show any reason as such, it says try again later. It is pretty straight forward process I believe as I am following steps as per the tutorial. Is there any prerequisite to be able to get it working?

entry.title: Running SAP Build Automation BOT in Unattended Mode
entry.content:
Hi SAP Build Automation Experts,

We have developed a BOT that performs a SAPUI5 process (click on web components and so on) This process was recorded using the "Screen recorder". currently running on a VDI provided by our customer. However, we are encountering issues when attempting to run the BOT in unattended mode. The BOT fails to run when no one is logged into the VDI, but it runs smoothly when any developer is logged in.

According to the documentation, there are two main options for running UI BOTs: Unattended Mode and Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). Unfortunately, it's unclear to us when each option should be used and what the key differences are.

Could someone advise us on the appropriate use cases for each option and why one might be preferred over the other?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Review old attachments in Process Visibility
entry.content:
Hi,

We're running some tests with Build Process Automation and didn't manage to find any functionality to see the attachments included in the process after it was finished.

The general idea here would be either to have them accessible in the visibility flow instead of having to build something that would access the SAP DMS by APIs instead.

It seems we are missing something obvious here, as this seems to be quite the basic funcionality.  Basically what we are doing is adding an attachment to a an approval step(e.g., a contract modification) and want to be able to review later what was actually atached but the visibility step shows nothing and we cannot add the attachments as part of the visibility itself. Has anyone faced this issue?

Rgds,

Adriano

entry.title: How to resolve resnponse from a Web Service call in a Build Process Automation task
entry.content:
Hello,

I am calling a web service with destination task in an autmation and need to extract information from the body of the response object.

Getting Goods Receipt details

Get the following response. Need to extrat the value of QuantityInBaseUnit.

entry.title: showAdditionalAttributes does not work for SBPA Process UI task
entry.content:
Dear SAP team,

i tried using the showAdditionalAttributes property of My inbox app in BTP work zone site to display additional task attributes defined on SBPA process user task ,but somehow this does not display this information ,is this feature supported now ?,documentation does not say anything about this feature now but inbox app has this property set as false .

Configure the My Inbox App | SAP Help Portal

if this is not supported then what is the purpose of defining the attributes at task level i am unable to understand.

Any quick response is more than welcome.

Thanks,
Siddharth

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Business Technology Platform SAP Workflow Management, workflow capability

entry.title: BAPI exception : Prerequisites are missing
entry.content:
Hi,

When using Set SAP Connection (Basic) BAPI activity in sap build process automation i am getting this error "BAPI exception: Prerequisites are missing". Can you please let me know what exactly i need to do now to overcome this error and make a successful connection with SAP through BAPI.....

entry.title: Grant users read-only access to the context of a sap build process automation workflow
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently facing the issue, that I want to grant read-only access to the context of a sap process automation (SBPA) workflow to my end users, via a REST call. Hereby, I find myself in the following scenario. I created a custom UI5-Application that uses the instance-id of a workflow to display the context. Of course, I want to design the authorization in such a way that the user is not able to manipulate the call to the SBPA API by,  for example, sending a post request via Postman.

To implement this, I have evaluated the following options, which have not led to a satisfactory result:

  • Create a destination with client id and client secret of the SBPA service intance
    • By doing this, I enable the user to access the context but also to take the called URL and send a post request, even though the actual credentials are concealed in the destination. 
  • Use API Key's within SBPA
    • I was not able to get them to grant access at all. In addition, I am unsettled by the following statement in this documentation on API keys: "You only need to use API keys for triggers that run automations."

Maybe I am missing something or one of you has another idea and can guide me into the right direction.

Thanks in advance,
Lukas

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - Dynamic Form Field Visibility
entry.content:
Hi Experts

is it possible to dynamically control the visibility or "Read Only" Mode for a field in a Form based on the value of another field.

In given example I would like to enable/disable the field "textDynamicOnOff" based on the selection of "Checkbox OnOff".

 

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: MyInbox in the SAP Build Work Zone lists tasks, but does not display them.
entry.content:
I have subscribet to SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Workzone in the same Subaccount. 

On my Work Zone Site i added the Process Trigger and MyInbox component. The Process Trigger works fine but MyInbox isn't working correctly. It lists the assigend Task of an User on the left menue but nothing happens when i click on them to work on the forms.

I created an Destination on the subaccount für SAP Build Process Automation in preparation for MyInbox on Work Zone.

 

SAP Build Process Automation SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

entry.title: How to integrate SAP Datasphere Consumption API in SAP BTP Destination and SAP BTP-BPA
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing an issue when creating a destination on Sap data sphere Consumption API. I am getting a 401 authorization issue when creating a Destination using Oauth 2.0. However, using the same URL in Postman is working fine.

Can you suggest a solution to this issue?

This is the authorization I am using for Postman.

entry.title: Workzone BPA task error "Failed to resolve dependencies of 'CrossApplicationNavigation.js'"
entry.content:
Dear Community,
since Monday I can’t see custom tasks in Workzone in some clients' sub-accounts.

The error I see in the browser console is:
Failed to resolve dependencies of 'sap/ushell/appRuntime/ui5/services/CrossApplicationNavigation.js'

Tasks are correctly viewable on use MyInbox of BPA.

Does anyone else have this error?

@Archana Could it be due to some update over the weekend?

Best Regards
